meaning: Moses (a name meaning 'drawn from water' or 'child of')
from them (referring to the group of people Pharaoh oppressed)
their sons (the sons of the people Pharaoh was oppressing)
they were made weak (referring to those who were oppressed)
leaders (as the object being made in this verse)
then/so when (connects this future event to what was mentioned before)
the ones who are sent (plural, accusative)
and then he picked him up
Pharaoh (as the object being spoken about)
for/to them (referring to the family of Pharaoh)
you all kill him (referring to baby Musa)
they sense (referring to the disbelievers in the story)
follow him closely (command to Moses' sister)
with/by him (referring to the baby Moses)
upon him (referring to the nursing child in the story)
I guide you all (referring to the people being addressed in this ayah)
and yet/however (connecting to previous statement about Allah's promise being true)
most of them (referring to the people who don't know Allah's promise is true)
and when (indicates a sequence of events)
so he sought his help (after finding the two men fighting)
so he ended it (referring to the fight)
indeed he (referring to Allah being forgiving)
he (referring to Allah in this context)
The Most Forgiving (one of Allah's names)
The Intensely Merciful (one of Allah's names)
he watches himself carefully (while fearful in the city)
he sought his help (referring to the person who had helped him the day before)
he seeks help from him (the one who sought his help yesterday)
to/for him (the one who asked for help yesterday)
you killed (referring to what Musa did)
the people who make things better
so exit! (as a result of what was mentioned)
from it/her (referring to the city he left)
a community/nation (that he found at the water of Madyan)
besides them (referring to the people who were watering)
he makes (something) return (until the shepherds finish)
for both of them (referring to the two women he watered for)
any good thing (that You send down to me)
and then she came to him
one of them both (referring to the two daughters)
so that he may give you what you deserve
the people (who are being fled from)
one of (referring to one of his two daughters)
eight (as a counted number in this ayah)
you will find me (speaking to Allah)
that (referring to what was just mentioned)
a guardian, protector (witness to what we say)
he noticed/perceived (referring to Moses noticing the fire)
the valley (the specific valley on the blessed side mentioned in this ayah)
the blessed spot/place (location where Allah spoke to Moses)
of the tree (in the blessed spot)
indeed I (speaking about myself with emphasis)
he saw her/it (referring to the staff)
a snake (like the staff appeared to be)
so these two (referring to the miracles of the shining hand and staff)
those who break away from obedience
from them (referring to the people who killed someone from among them)
in tongue/speech (specifies how Harun is more eloquent)
with me (referring to Moses speaking about himself)
for/to both of you (referring to Moses and Aaron)
a strong authority/clear proof (as the object being given)
they said (referring to those who rejected Moses's signs)
except (what comes after is excluded from what was said before)
the wrongdoers (those who will not succeed)
I knew (what Pharaoh is saying to his people)
the liars (referring to specific group of liars)
and he tried to make himself seem greater
of the fire (calling to the fire)
the worldly life (this life as opposed to the afterlife)
they/them (referring to the disbelievers mentioned before)
the despicable ones (those who are cast away from Allah's mercy)
the generations (that were destroyed before Moses)
Moses (the prophet being addressed in this ayah)
their hands (what they have done with their own hands)
you sent (addressing someone in this ayah)
with what/that which (referring to what was given to Moses)
Moses (the prophet mentioned in the Quran)
two magics (referring to Torah and Quran that the disbelievers called magic)
he caused to be guided/he made guide
he followed intensively (by his own choice)
the wrongdoers/oppressors (who Allah does not guide)
so that they might/perhaps they will
indeed it/he (referring to what was recited to them)
our Lord (in this ayah: speaking to Allah)
and they said (connecting to what came before)
with you (in the phrase 'if we follow the guidance with you')
they know (referring to those who don't follow guidance)
so those (referring to the dwellings mentioned before)
after them (referring to the people of the destroyed towns)
a little while (describing how long the houses remained inhabited)
those who act unjustly (referring to multiple people)
we indeed gave him much enjoyment
he (referring to the one who was promised something good in this ayah)
you all claim without proof (addressing the disbelievers who claimed false partners with Allah)
we disassociated ourselves (from those we led astray)
so they called them (after being told to call their partners)
what? (asking about their response to the messengers)
and so it (the news/information) became unclear
on that day (when the news was unclear to them)
for them (the choice belongs to them)
of the deity, of the god
their chests (what they conceal in their hearts)
he comes/brings to you all (addressing multiple people)
eternally (describing how Allah makes the day continue)
so that you all may find rest
in it (referring to night and day)
and so that you all seek (from His bounty)
my partners (that Allah is asking about)
you (all) claim without proof (addressing the disbelievers)
a witness (being mentioned in this statement)
so they knew (after what was mentioned before)
from them (referring to what they used to invent/fabricate)
he said (introducing what his people told him)
the excessively joyful ones (who Allah does not love)
Allah (The One True God who is the source of all goodness to you)
you forget (as a command not to)
and do good! (command to one person, connected to previous command)
I was given it (referring to wealth/provision mentioned earlier)
who (is stronger than him in power)
from him (referring to the knowledge mentioned before)
in gathering (describing what they were more abundant in)
they want/desire (referring to those who said the previous statement)
was given (referring to what Qarun was given)
is made to receive it (referring to the reward mentioned earlier)
and so we sank (something) into the ground
they wished something for themselves intensely
if not, had it not been
he would surely have made (something) sink
woe! it is as if he/it
that (feminine, pointing to something far away)
they want/desire (referring to those who don't seek elevation or corruption)
for the ones who guard against evil
then for him (referring to whoever brings a good deed)
he is given what is due
the one who/that which - relative pronoun that connects a description to what is being described
he made required (the Quran upon you)
place of return (being described in this ayah)
more knowing (than those who are misguided)
you will definitely be (a helper for the disbelievers)
the polytheists (those who join partners with Allah)
except (in this ayah: everything will perish except His Face)
His Face (referring to Allah's Face, being excluded from destruction)
